extraction.one_line_summary: DEGENT Thomas attacked MOHORIČ Matej on the final climb of a 5-lap circuit with 3km to go, exploiting Mohorič's tactical decision to keep pulling despite having teammate SOLER Marc in the chase group.
tactical_patterns: Movistar let 5-minute break with multiple own riders go instead of chasing for VALVERDE stage win/podium despite past success here.
MOHORIČ waited for DEGENT after dropping him every descent, telegraphing weakness and enabling final climb counter-attack with 3km remaining.
INEOS used CARAPAZ Richard as domestique to police climbers despite being Giro champion, revealing hierarchy prioritization over individual ambitions.
Lotto-Soudal bookended race with stage wins (KRON, DEGENT) via breakaway control, not GC firepower, signaling viable alternative tactical model.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: First World Tour GC win in Europe, improved TT losing only 7s to Almeida on stage 2 versus 30s+ to Pogačar in UAE Tour
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Last-kilometer surge on climbs showing 2018-level form, concerning signal for Tour rivals if he maintains trajectory
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: At 41, still Movistar's strongest climber per stage 3 performance, warranted more team commitment than SOLER breakaway play
rider_signals.MOHORIČ Matej: negative: Tactical passivity waiting for DEGENT on descents despite superior descending, should have forced harder pace knowing KELDERMAN chase threatened
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Reigning Giro champion relegated to policing breakaways (stage 4 mass chase), signals INEOS views him as super-domestique not co-leader
